note: Tightly-coupled redesign — every AC depends on hook + 5 SKILL.md state-write recipes + project.json + tests being in place together. One scenario + one implement pass.

recipe.name: test_stop_hook_emits_block_when_state_is_continue_and_marker_present
recipe.assertion: Given state=continue + marker present + stop_hook_active absent, the hook emits decision block JSON to stdout and exits 0.

recipe.name: test_stop_hook_silent_when_marker_absent
recipe.assertion: Given state=continue but no .harness_active on disk, the hook stays silent (exit 0, stdout empty).
recipe.fixtures: createTempProject; writeHarnessState continue; do NOT create marker; invokeHook.

recipe.name: test_stop_hook_silent_when_stop_hook_active_true
recipe.assertion: Given stop_hook_active=true in payload, the hook stays silent regardless of marker/state.
recipe.fixtures: createTempProject; writeHarnessState continue; create marker; invokeHook({stop_hook_active:true}).

recipe.name: test_stop_hook_silent_when_harness_state_missing
recipe.assertion: When harness_state file is absent, hook stays silent.

recipe.name: test_stop_hook_silent_when_harness_state_malformed_json
recipe.assertion: When harness_state contains malformed JSON, hook stays silent.
recipe.fixtures: createTempProject; fs.writeFile harness_state with 'not parseable json {{{'; invokeHook.

recipe.name: test_stop_hook_silent_when_state_is_yielded
recipe.assertion: Given state=yielded, hook stays silent even if marker happens to be present.
recipe.fixtures: createTempProject; writeHarnessState({state:'yielded',...}); optionally create marker; invokeHook.

recipe.name: test_stop_hook_logs_warn_on_slug_mismatch
recipe.assertion: When marker content slug disagrees with workflow.json slug, hook appends a WARN line to harness_continuation.log AND still emits block (decision unchanged).
recipe.fixtures: createTempProject; writeHarnessState({state:'continue', slug:'right'}); marker contents 'wrong\n'; workflow.json slug='right'; invokeHook. Assert: stdout JSON .decision='block' AND tail of harness_continuation.log contains 'WARN' + 'slug mismatch' + 'wrong' + 'right'.

recipe.name: test_stop_hook_chain_within_turn
recipe.assertion: Two consecutive invocations: first with stop_hook_active=false emits block; second with stop_hook_active=true stays silent — preserving the in-turn recursion guard.
recipe.fixtures: createTempProject; writeHarnessState continue; create marker; invokeHook({stop_hook_active:false}) then invokeHook({stop_hook_active:true}).

recipe.name: test_memory_session_start_removes_stale_marker
recipe.covers: AC-003 (NEW, in new describe block 'memory_session_start marker cleanup')
recipe.assertion: Given a stale .harness_active on disk, when memory_session_start.sh runs, the marker is removed AND a one-line INFO entry is appended to harness_continuation.log containing the slug that was removed.
recipe.fixtures: createTempProject; fs.writeFile('.claude/state/.harness_active', 'stale-slug\n'); execFileSync('bash', ['.claude/hooks/memory_session_start.sh'], {input: JSON.stringify({source:'startup'}), env:{CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR: tmp}, ...}). Assert: marker gone AND log line contains 'INFO' + 'removed stale .harness_active' + 'stale-slug'.

recipe.name: test_harness_state_is_3_fields_only
recipe.assertion: After harness skill writes harness_state per its documented recipe, the JSON keys are exactly {state, slug, reason} (no written_at, no tick_count).
recipe.fixtures: Read .claude/state/harness_state from the dev repo (this workflow's state); JSON.parse; assert Object.keys(parsed).sort() deepEqual ['reason','slug','state']. NOTE: this test is expected to FAIL initially against the dev-repo state file (which currently has 5 fields from in-flight workflows) — that's the failing test. Implementation makes it pass by having the harness skill write only 3 fields going forward.

recipe.name: test_project_json_has_no_harness_key
recipe.assertion: After the redesign lands, .claude/project.json has no top-level 'harness' key.

out_of_scope_scenarios: Performance/latency tests (hook overhead already measured: <500ms; no SLA in scope).
session_id-based fallback (Option A) — rejected in research.
Transcript-walking fallback (Option B) — rejected in research.
Backward-compatibility migration tooling for old harness_state shape — forward-compat is symmetric and runtime-state files are overwritten on every tick.

behavior_excerpts: spec §Behavior 1 (continue tick): harness writes marker FIRST then harness_state; Stop hook 3-rung gate passes; emits block; in-turn recursion guard handles second fire.
spec §Behavior 2 (yield tick): harness deletes marker FIRST then writes state=yielded; Stop hook Rung 2 sees marker absent; silent.
spec §Behavior 3 (SessionStart ghost prevention): memory_session_start.sh rm -f marker unconditionally + log INFO line; next user prompt's Stop event sees marker absent; silent.
spec Class diagram: HarnessState = {state, slug, reason} only (3 fields).
spec Class diagram: HarnessActiveMarker = file existence + slug content; created on continue / deleted on yielded|done.
spec Class diagram: ProjectConfig — removed: harness.continue_window_seconds, harness.max_ticks_per_session.

project_conventions.hook_style: bash + python3 stdlib heredoc; no jq, no Node
project_conventions.test_style: node:test + node:assert/strict; existing fixtures createTempProject/writeHarnessState/invokeHook/defaultPayload at tests/harness_continuation.test.mjs:1-60
project_conventions.ordering_rule: marker FIRST, harness_state SECOND on every state-write boundary (continue OR yielded/done)

implementation_order: 5. CLAUDE.md + src/CLAUDE.template.md — Article V + Article VIII edits, byte-equal mirror
6. docs/init/seed.md + src/seed.template.md — §4.1 intro + §4.1 row + §7 auto-continuation, §16 reservation preserved
8. tests/harness_continuation.test.mjs — delete 2, rewrite 1, add 6 (5 hook tests + 1 SessionStart cleanup test in new describe)
